title: Column-based contextual menu with form element to add commands to a search query
abstract: A search interface is displayed in a table format that includes a plurality of columns, each column including data items of an event attribute, the data items being of a set of events, each column being selectable by a user, and a plurality of rows forming cells with the one or more columns, each cell comprising one or more of the data items of the event attribute of a corresponding column. Based on the user selecting one or more of the columns, a list of options is displayed corresponding to the selected one or more columns, and one or more commands are added to a search query that corresponds to the set of events. The one or more commands are based on at least an option that is selected from the list of options and the event attribute of each of the selected one or more columns.
claims: 1. A computer-implemented method, comprising: causing display of a table comprising rows of search results matching a search query; in response to a first click or touch on a column header on a selected column within the table, causing display of a contextual menu of selectable options that correspond with commands to update the search query and are contextually based on an attribute of the search results that is represented by the selected column, one of the selectable options in the contextual menu being displayed as a form with a text box operable to accept a textual entry; and based at least on receiving the textual entry into the text box in the contextual menu, configuring the search query to update the search results in the table by causing one or more commands that are based on the selectable option, the textual entry, and the attribute to be added to the search query.
